title: Self-exciting pit-wave intensity features
summary: Pit stops cluster in waves around undercut/overcut windows, VSC/SC reactions, and compound crossover moments.
rationale: Pit stops cluster in waves around undercut/overcut windows, VSC/SC reactions, and compound crossover moments. Self-exciting point-process literature is built for event clustering, and here a lightweight approximation can encode how recent stops by nearby competitors raise immediate stop probability.
implementation_hint: Create decayed counts of pit stops in the previous 1-3 laps for the same race, split by position bands, same compound, and same stint age bucket; also add race-lap shock features like sudden field-wide pace variance jumps interacting with those decayed counts.
expected_effect: Should capture contagious stop waves better than static race-lap aggregates and improve ranking within crowded decision windows.
risk: Can overlap with prior pit-wave ideas; needs careful ablation to prove the explicit decay kernels add signal beyond simple race-lap pit rates.
